analysis.summary: A partial U.S. government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security has become the longest in American history, surpassing 43-44 days, causing significant TSA staffing shortages and long airport security lines. The funding lapse began after Senate Democrats blocked a DHS funding bill, demanding reforms to ICE and Border Patrol.
